short_description: This is a dorsal view showing the top side of the foot bones, displaying the metatarsals and phalanges of all five toes with the proximal portions of the metatarsals visible at the bottom of the image.

View from the bottom or sole side of the foot, showing the plantar surface of the metatarsals and phalanges with the calcaneus and tarsal bones visible proximally. The five metatarsal heads and toe bones are clearly visible from their inferior aspect.

Medial view of the foot bones showing the big-toe side, with the calcaneus and tarsal bones visible at the proximal end, the metatarsals in the midfoot, and the phalanges extending distally. The medial longitudinal arch is clearly visible from this perspective.

This lateral view shows the outer (little-toe) side of the foot bones, displaying the metatarsals, cuboid, and calcaneus with clear visualization of the longitudinal arch structure. The fifth metatarsal and lateral tarsal bones are prominently visible from this perspective.

View from the toe-end of the foot looking toward the ankle, showing the distal ends of the metatarsals and phalanges. The five rays of the toes are visible from their tips, displaying the rounded articular surfaces and distal phalanges.

View from the ankle-end of the foot bones, showing the proximal articular surfaces of the metatarsals and tarsals. The bases of all five metatarsals are visible with the tarsal bones arranged behind them.
View of the foot bones from the top and big-toe side, showing the dorsal surface of the metatarsals, cuneiforms, and phalanges with clear visibility of the medial column and first ray structures.

View of the foot bones from the top and little-toe side, showing the metatarsals and phalanges with the dorsal surface and lateral aspect visible. The bones are arranged from the first through fifth rays, displaying their natural curvature and articulations.
View from the top and toe-end of the foot, showing the dorsal surface of the metatarsals and phalanges with clear visualization of the toe bones and their articulations.
View from the top side of the foot looking toward the ankle and heel area, showing the metatarsals and phalanges from above with the proximal tarsal bones visible in the background.

View of the foot bones from the plantar (sole) and medial (big-toe) side, showing the underside and inner aspect of the foot. The calcaneus, midfoot bones, metatarsals, and phalanges are visible from this combined plantar-medial perspective.

View of the foot bones from the plantar (sole) side with emphasis on the lateral (little-toe) aspect. The metatarsals, phalanges, and tarsal bones are visible from below and from the outer side of the foot.

View of the foot bones from the plantar (sole) side looking from the distal (toe) end toward the proximal (heel) end. The metatarsals and phalanges of all five toes are clearly visible from below, showing the bottom surface anatomy of the forefoot.
View of the foot bones from the plantar (sole) side, angled from the proximal (heel) direction, showing the underside of the metatarsals and phalanges with the toes visible in the foreground.
View from the big-toe side and toe-end of the foot, showing the metatarsals and phalanges extending toward the viewer with the midfoot and hindfoot bones visible in the background.

View from the big-toe side and ankle-end of the foot, showing the medial longitudinal arch, tarsal bones, and metatarsals. The calcaneus and talus are prominently visible from the proximal perspective.

View from the little-toe side of the foot looking toward the toe-end, showing the lateral aspect of the metatarsals and phalanges. The fifth metatarsal and lateral foot bones are prominently visible from this oblique distal perspective.

View of the foot bones from the little-toe side angled toward the ankle end, showing the lateral metatarsals, cuboid, and calcaneus. The fifth metatarsal and lateral tarsal bones are prominently displayed.
